sections.paragraph:
εὐκταῖος, α, ον pert. to being an object of petition, prayed for, and what is prayed for may be said to be wished for (so Aeschyl., Pla.+; PGiss 68, 3 [II a.d.]; Philo; Jos., Ant. 1, 292 al.; Mel., HE 4, 26, 7) εἰρήνη 1 Cl 65:1 (w. ἐπιπόθητος).—DELG s.v. εὔχομαι.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 407
